California SCM — interactive training & analysis¶

Run cells top-to-bottom for a full training run.
Re-run individual cells to iterate without retraining (guide/model stay in memory).

In [42]:
import sys, json
from pathlib import Path

ROOT = Path('..').resolve()
sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT))

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import torch
import pyro
import matplotlib
%matplotlib inline
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.ticker as mticker

from model.scm import make_tensors, make_model, train, predict, mape
from model.train_california import priors_from_data, peak_metrics, print_peak_summary
from data.ca_pipeline import (
    load, train_test_split, label_spikes, match_normals, make_scenario, SCENARIOS
)
from model.counterfactual import (
    decompose_hours, uplift_decomposition, peak_scenario_summary,
    COMPONENTS, COMPONENT_LABELS
)
from analysis.spike_report import (
    plot_actual_vs_predicted, plot_weather_uplift,
    plot_spike_decomposition, plot_scenario_impact,
    print_uplift_summary, print_scenario_summary
)

DATA_DIR    = ROOT / 'data' / 'california'
RESULTS_DIR = ROOT / 'results' / 'california'
RESULTS_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
(RESULTS_DIR / 'figures').mkdir(exist_ok=True)

print('imports ok')

imports ok

1 — Load data¶

In [43]:
df = load(DATA_DIR / 'ca_merged.csv')
df_train, df_test = train_test_split(df)

print(f"Train: {len(df_train):,} rows  "
      f"{df_train['period'].min().date()} – {df_train['period'].max().date()}")
print(f"Test:  {len(df_test):,} rows  "
      f"{df_test['period'].min().date()} – {df_test['period'].max().date()}")
df_train.describe()

Train: 8,614 rows  2023-09-08 – 2024-08-31
Test:  8,760 rows  2024-09-01 – 2025-08-31

2 — Estimate priors¶

In [44]:
train_priors = priors_from_data(df_train)
for name, (mu, sig) in train_priors.items():
    mu_str = f'{mu:.1f}' if not isinstance(mu, list) else '[...]'
    print(f'  {name:<14}: mean={mu_str}  std={sig}')

  E0            : mean=0.0  std=2000.0
  k_cool        : mean=60.0  std=50.0
  k_heat        : mean=15.0  std=20.0
  T_cool_base   : mean=65.0  std=4.0
  T_heat_base   : mean=55.0  std=4.0
  T_base        : mean=62.4  std=4.0
  a             : mean=[...]  std=40.0
  alpha         : mean=[...]  std=40.0
  mu_rh_base    : mean=64.9  std=20.0
  mu_w_base     : mean=5.1  std=10.0
  rad_base      : mean=219.2  std=50.0

3 — Train¶

Adjust NUM_STEPS to trade speed for accuracy.
Re-running this cell resets the param store and trains from scratch.

In [45]:
NUM_STEPS = 10_000

ca_model = make_model(train_priors)
tensors_train = make_tensors(df_train)
tensors_test  = make_tensors(df_test)

svi, guide = train(tensors_train, model=ca_model, num_steps=NUM_STEPS)
print('Training complete.')

Training SCM for 10000 steps ...

  step  8000  ELBO loss = 222356.6
  step  8500  ELBO loss = 217350.6

  step 10000  ELBO loss = 216102.4
Training complete.

4 — Predict & metrics¶

Safe to re-run after training without retraining.

In [46]:
y_train_pred = predict(guide, tensors_train, model=ca_model)
y_test_pred  = predict(guide, tensors_test,  model=ca_model)

df_train_pred = df_train.assign(pred_mwh=y_train_pred)
df_test_pred  = df_test.assign(pred_mwh=y_test_pred)

train_pm = peak_metrics(df_train_pred, label='train')
test_pm  = peak_metrics(df_test_pred,  label='test')

print_peak_summary(train_pm, 'Train')
print_peak_summary(test_pm,  'Test')

  Train
    All-hour MAPE         : 4.94%
    Top-1% load MAPE      : 5.16%
    Top-5% load MAPE      : 5.21%
    Monthly peak MAPE     : 3.48%
    Annual peak error     : 1.43%
    Peak underforecast    : 89.7% of top-1% hours

  Test
    All-hour MAPE         : 5.06%
    Top-1% load MAPE      : 9.53%
    Top-5% load MAPE      : 6.65%
    Monthly peak MAPE     : 4.59%
    Annual peak error     : 10.44%
    Peak underforecast    : 100.0% of top-1% hours
In [47]:
# Save predictions CSV and param store
df_out = pd.concat([
    df_train.assign(pred_mwh=y_train_pred, split='train'),
    df_test.assign(pred_mwh=y_test_pred,   split='test'),
])
df_out.to_csv(RESULTS_DIR / 'predictions.csv', index=False)
pyro.get_param_store().save(str(RESULTS_DIR / 'param_store.pt'))
print('Saved predictions.csv and param_store.pt')

Saved predictions.csv and param_store.pt

5 — Quick plots¶

In [48]:
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 1, figsize=(14, 8), gridspec_kw={'hspace': 0.4})

# top: test time series
test_sorted = df_test_pred.sort_values('period')
ax = axes[0]
ax.plot(test_sorted['period'], test_sorted['demand_mwh'], lw=0.5, alpha=0.7, label='Actual')
ax.plot(test_sorted['period'], test_sorted['pred_mwh'],  lw=0.5, alpha=0.7, label='Predicted')
ax.set_title('Test Period — Actual vs Predicted', fontsize=11)
ax.set_ylabel('Demand (MW)')
ax.legend(fontsize=8)
ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f'{x/1000:.0f}K'))

# bottom: monthly peak bar
test_sorted['ym'] = test_sorted['period'].dt.to_period('M')
mp = test_sorted.groupby('ym')[['demand_mwh','pred_mwh']].max().reset_index()
x, w = np.arange(len(mp)), 0.35
ax2 = axes[1]
ax2.bar(x-w/2, mp['demand_mwh']/1000, w, label='Actual peak', color='steelblue', alpha=0.85)
ax2.bar(x+w/2, mp['pred_mwh']/1000,   w, label='Predicted peak', color='coral', alpha=0.85)
ax2.set_xticks(x)
ax2.set_xticklabels([str(ym) for ym in mp['ym']], rotation=45, ha='right', fontsize=8)
ax2.set_title('Monthly Peaks', fontsize=11)
ax2.set_ylabel('Peak (GW)')
ax2.legend(fontsize=8)
plt.show()
In [49]:
# MAPE by hour-of-day
df_test_pred['ape'] = (df_test_pred['pred_mwh'] - df_test_pred['demand_mwh']).abs() / df_test_pred['demand_mwh'] * 100
hourly_mape = df_test_pred.groupby('hour')['ape'].mean()
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 4))
plt.bar(hourly_mape.index, hourly_mape.values, color='steelblue', alpha=0.8)
plt.axhline(test_pm['all_hour_mape'], color='red', ls='--', lw=1, label=f"Overall {test_pm['all_hour_mape']:.2f}%")
plt.xlabel('Hour of Day'); plt.ylabel('MAPE (%)'); plt.title('Test MAPE by Hour')
plt.legend(); plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()

6 — Cross-validation¶

Slow — only run when you want CV estimates.

In [50]:
'''from sklearn.model_selection import TimeSeriesSplit

CV_STEPS = 10_000
N_SPLITS = 5

tscv = TimeSeriesSplit(n_splits=N_SPLITS)
all_tr_sizes = [len(tr) for tr, _ in tscv.split(df_train)]
ref_size = all_tr_sizes[0]

fold_metrics_list = []
for fold, (tr_idx, val_idx) in enumerate(tscv.split(df_train), 1):
    tr  = df_train.iloc[tr_idx].reset_index(drop=True)
    val = df_train.iloc[val_idx].reset_index(drop=True)
    fold_steps  = int(CV_STEPS * len(tr_idx) / ref_size)
    fold_priors = priors_from_data(tr)
    fold_model  = make_model(fold_priors)
    _, fold_guide = train(make_tensors(tr), model=fold_model, num_steps=fold_steps)
    y_pred = predict(fold_guide, make_tensors(val), model=fold_model)
    fm = peak_metrics(val.assign(pred_mwh=y_pred), label=f'cv_fold_{fold}')
    fold_metrics_list.append(fm)
    print(f"  Fold {fold}  train={len(tr):,}  val={len(val):,}  steps={fold_steps:,}  "
          f"MAPE={fm['all_hour_mape']:.2f}%  top1={fm['top1_mape']:.2f}%  "
          f"monthly_peak={fm['monthly_peak_mape']:.2f}%")

cv_mapes = [fm['all_hour_mape'] for fm in fold_metrics_list]
print(f"\nCV mean MAPE: {np.mean(cv_mapes):.2f}%  (±{np.std(cv_mapes):.2f}%)")'''
Out[50]:
'from sklearn.model_selection import TimeSeriesSplit\n\nCV_STEPS = 10_000\nN_SPLITS = 5\n\ntscv = TimeSeriesSplit(n_splits=N_SPLITS)\nall_tr_sizes = [len(tr) for tr, _ in tscv.split(df_train)]\nref_size = all_tr_sizes[0]\n\nfold_metrics_list = []\nfor fold, (tr_idx, val_idx) in enumerate(tscv.split(df_train), 1):\n    tr  = df_train.iloc[tr_idx].reset_index(drop=True)\n    val = df_train.iloc[val_idx].reset_index(drop=True)\n    fold_steps  = int(CV_STEPS * len(tr_idx) / ref_size)\n    fold_priors = priors_from_data(tr)\n    fold_model  = make_model(fold_priors)\n    _, fold_guide = train(make_tensors(tr), model=fold_model, num_steps=fold_steps)\n    y_pred = predict(fold_guide, make_tensors(val), model=fold_model)\n    fm = peak_metrics(val.assign(pred_mwh=y_pred), label=f\'cv_fold_{fold}\')\n    fold_metrics_list.append(fm)\n    print(f"  Fold {fold}  train={len(tr):,}  val={len(val):,}  steps={fold_steps:,}  "\n          f"MAPE={fm[\'all_hour_mape\']:.2f}%  top1={fm[\'top1_mape\']:.2f}%  "\n          f"monthly_peak={fm[\'monthly_peak_mape\']:.2f}%")\n\ncv_mapes = [fm[\'all_hour_mape\'] for fm in fold_metrics_list]\nprint(f"\nCV mean MAPE: {np.mean(cv_mapes):.2f}%  (±{np.std(cv_mapes):.2f}%)")'

7 — Component decomposition¶

Requires a trained guide in memory (cell 3).

In [51]:
print('Decomposing test hours into SCM components (200 posterior samples) ...')
test_decomp = decompose_hours(guide, ca_model, df_test, num_samples=200)
test_decomp.to_csv(RESULTS_DIR / 'decomposition.csv', index=False)
print(f'Saved decomposition.csv  ({len(test_decomp):,} rows)')
test_decomp[[c for c in COMPONENTS if c in test_decomp.columns] + ['pred_mwh','demand_mwh']].describe().round(1)

Decomposing test hours into SCM components (200 posterior samples) ...
Saved decomposition.csv  (8,760 rows)

In [52]:
# Stacked bar: mean component contribution per hour-of-day
td = test_decomp.copy()
td['hour'] = pd.to_datetime(td['period']).dt.hour
comp_cols = [c for c in COMPONENTS if c in td.columns]
hourly_comp = td.groupby('hour')[comp_cols].mean()

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 6))
bottom = np.zeros(24)
colors = plt.cm.tab20.colors
for i, col in enumerate(comp_cols):
    vals = hourly_comp[col].values
    ax.bar(hourly_comp.index, vals, bottom=bottom,
           label=COMPONENT_LABELS.get(col, col), color=colors[i % 20], alpha=0.85)
    bottom += vals
ax.set_xlabel('Hour of Day'); ax.set_ylabel('MW contribution')
ax.set_title('Mean Demand Component by Hour (Test Period)')
ax.legend(fontsize=7, loc='upper left', ncol=2)
plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()

8 — Spike vs. normal uplift decomposition¶

In [53]:
df_test_labeled = label_spikes(df_test_pred)
spike_mask = df_test_labeled['is_top1pct']
print(f'Spike hours (top 1%): {spike_mask.sum()}')

spike_decomp  = decompose_hours(guide, ca_model,
                                df_test_labeled[spike_mask].reset_index(drop=True))
normal_matched = match_normals(df_test_labeled, spike_mask)
print(f'Matched normal hours: {len(normal_matched)}')

normal_decomp = decompose_hours(guide, ca_model, normal_matched.reset_index(drop=True))
uplift = uplift_decomposition(spike_decomp, normal_decomp)
uplift.to_csv(RESULTS_DIR / 'spike_decomposition.csv', index=False)
print_uplift_summary(uplift)

Spike hours (top 1%): 88
Matched normal hours: 946

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Spike vs Normal Demand Uplift  (total: +10,365 MW)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Component                      Uplift MW  % of total
  Lag demand                        +5,906       57.0%  ▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓
  Cooling (linear)                    +397        3.8%  ▓
  Inland extreme heat                  +30        0.3%  
  Daily shape                          +13        0.1%  
  Holiday                               +0        0.0%  
  Baseline (E0)                         -0       -0.0%  
  Day-of-week                           -1       -0.0%  
  Wind                                  -1       -0.0%  
  Cooling (nonlinear)                   -1       -0.0%  
  Heating                               -3       -0.0%  
  Lighting                             -15       -0.1%  
  Heat persistence                     -31       -0.3%  
  Humidity                             -45       -0.4%  
  Seasonal                            -216       -2.1%
In [54]:
# Horizontal bar chart
u = uplift.sort_values('uplift_mw', ascending=True)
colors_bar = ['#d62728' if v > 0 else '#1f77b4' for v in u['uplift_mw']]
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))
bars = ax.barh(u['label'], u['uplift_mw'], color=colors_bar, alpha=0.85)
ax.axvline(0, color='black', lw=0.8)
ax.set_title('Spike vs Normal: Demand Uplift by Component', fontsize=11)
ax.set_xlabel('Mean MW difference (spike − normal)')
for bar, val in zip(bars, u['uplift_mw']):
    ax.text(val + (50 if val >= 0 else -50), bar.get_y() + bar.get_height()/2,
            f'{val:+.0f} MW', va='center', ha='left' if val >= 0 else 'right', fontsize=8)
plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()

8b — Uplift decomposition with counterfactual lag¶

Replace each spike hour's E_lag24 / E_lag168 with the mean lag from its matched normal hours, so weather-mediated lag persistence is re-attributed to the temperature components instead of "Lag demand".

In [55]:
# Build counterfactual spike DataFrame: replace lags with matched-normal means
normal_lag_means = (
    normal_matched
    .groupby(normal_matched['matched_spike_idx'])[['E_lag24', 'E_lag168']]
    .mean()
)

spike_rows = df_test_labeled[spike_mask].reset_index(drop=True)
spike_cf = spike_rows.copy()
spike_cf['E_lag24']  = normal_lag_means['E_lag24'].values
spike_cf['E_lag168'] = normal_lag_means['E_lag168'].values

spike_decomp_cf = decompose_hours(guide, ca_model, spike_cf)
uplift_cf = uplift_decomposition(spike_decomp_cf, normal_decomp)

# Side-by-side comparison table
compare = (
    uplift[['label', 'uplift_mw', 'uplift_pct_of_total']]
    .merge(
        uplift_cf[['label', 'uplift_mw', 'uplift_pct_of_total']],
        on='label', suffixes=('_observed_lag', '_cf_lag')
    )
    .sort_values('uplift_mw_observed_lag', ascending=False)
)
print("─" * 72)
print(f"  {'Component':<26}  {'Observed lag':>14}  {'CF lag':>14}  {'Δ':>8}")
print(f"  {'':26}  {'Uplift MW  %':>14}  {'Uplift MW  %':>14}  {'MW':>8}")
print("─" * 72)
for _, r in compare.iterrows():
    delta = r['uplift_mw_cf_lag'] - r['uplift_mw_observed_lag']
    print(f"  {r['label']:<26}  "
          f"{r['uplift_mw_observed_lag']:>+8,.0f}  {r['uplift_pct_of_total_observed_lag']:>4.1f}%  "
          f"{r['uplift_mw_cf_lag']:>+8,.0f}  {r['uplift_pct_of_total_cf_lag']:>4.1f}%  "
          f"{delta:>+8,.0f}")
print("─" * 72)

# Side-by-side bar charts
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 6), sharey=True)
for ax, (df_u, title) in zip(axes, [
    (uplift,    'Observed lag'),
    (uplift_cf, 'Counterfactual lag'),
]):
    u = df_u.sort_values('uplift_mw', ascending=True)
    colors_bar = ['#d62728' if v > 0 else '#1f77b4' for v in u['uplift_mw']]
    bars = ax.barh(u['label'], u['uplift_mw'], color=colors_bar, alpha=0.85)
    ax.axvline(0, color='black', lw=0.8)
    ax.set_title(title, fontsize=11)
    ax.set_xlabel('Mean MW difference (spike − normal)')
    for bar, val in zip(bars, u['uplift_mw']):
        ax.text(val + (30 if val >= 0 else -30),
                bar.get_y() + bar.get_height() / 2,
                f'{val:+.0f}', va='center',
                ha='left' if val >= 0 else 'right', fontsize=7)

fig.suptitle('Spike vs Normal Uplift: Observed vs Counterfactual Lag', fontsize=12)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Component                     Observed lag          CF lag         Δ
                                Uplift MW  %    Uplift MW  %        MW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Lag demand                    +5,906  57.0%      +195   1.9%    -5,710
  Cooling (linear)                +397   3.8%      +397   3.8%        -0
  Inland extreme heat              +30   0.3%       +30   0.3%        +0
  Daily shape                      +13   0.1%       +14   0.1%        +2
  Holiday                           +0   0.0%        +0   0.0%        +0
  Baseline (E0)                     -0  -0.0%        +0   0.0%        +0
  Day-of-week                       -1  -0.0%        -1  -0.0%        -1
  Wind                              -1  -0.0%        -1  -0.0%        +0
  Cooling (nonlinear)               -1  -0.0%        -1  -0.0%        +0
  Heating                           -3  -0.0%        -3  -0.0%        +0
  Lighting                         -15  -0.1%       -14  -0.1%        +1
  Heat persistence                 -31  -0.3%       -31  -0.3%        -0
  Humidity                         -45  -0.4%       -45  -0.4%        +0
  Seasonal                        -216  -2.1%      -217  -2.1%        -1
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

9 — Weather scenario analysis¶

In [56]:
print('Running weather scenarios (100 posterior samples each) ...')
cf_summary = peak_scenario_summary(guide, ca_model, df_test, SCENARIOS, num_samples=100)
cf_summary.to_csv(RESULTS_DIR / 'counterfactual_peaks.csv', index=False)
print_scenario_summary(cf_summary)
cf_summary

Running weather scenarios (100 posterior samples each) ...

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Scenario Analysis — Peak Demand Impact
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Scenario                     Peak MW    ΔPeak MW    Top-1% ΔMW
  observed                      42,571          +0            +0
  +2F                           42,453        -118           -21
  +5F                           42,494         -76           -26
  +10F                          42,890        +319          +140
  +5F_summer                    42,657         +86           +84

# Bar chart of scenario peak deltas
cf = cf_summary[cf_summary['scenario'] != 'observed'].copy()
cf = cf.sort_values('delta_peak_mw')
colors_cf = ['#d62728' if v > 0 else '#1f77b4' for v in cf['delta_peak_mw']]
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 5))
ax.barh(cf['scenario'], cf['delta_peak_mw'], color=colors_cf, alpha=0.85)
ax.axvline(0, color='black', lw=0.8)
ax.set_title('Scenario Analysis: ΔPeak Demand vs Observed Weather', fontsize=11)
ax.set_xlabel('ΔPeak (MW)')
plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()
In [58]:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches
import matplotlib.patheffects as pe
import networkx as nx

# Build DAG
G = nx.DiGraph()

nodes = {
    # Exogenous
    "Hour (h)":        "exog",
    "Month (m)":       "exog",
    # Weather
    "Temperature":     "weather",
    "Humidity":        "weather",
    "Wind":            "weather",
    "Solar radiation": "weather",
    # Lag
    "Lag demand\n(t-24h, t-168h)": "lag",
    # Demand components
    "Cooling":         "component",
    "Heating":         "component",
    "Inland heat":     "component",
    "Heat persistence":"component",
    "Humidity effect": "component",
    "Wind effect":     "component",
    "Daily shape":     "component",
    "Seasonal":        "component",
    "Lighting":        "component",
    "DOW / Holiday":   "component",
    "Baseline (E0)":   "component",
    # Output
    "Demand (E)":      "output",
}

edges = [
    ("Hour (h)",        "Temperature"),
    ("Month (m)",       "Temperature"),
    ("Hour (h)",        "Solar radiation"),
    ("Month (m)",       "Solar radiation"),
    ("Month (m)",       "Humidity"),
    ("Month (m)",       "Wind"),
    ("Temperature",     "Cooling"),
    ("Temperature",     "Heating"),
    ("Temperature",     "Inland heat"),
    ("Temperature",     "Humidity effect"),
    ("Temperature",     "Wind effect"),
    ("Humidity",        "Humidity effect"),
    ("Wind",            "Wind effect"),
    ("Solar radiation", "Lighting"),
    ("Hour (h)",        "Lighting"),
    ("Hour (h)",        "Daily shape"),
    ("Month (m)",       "Seasonal"),
    ("Hour (h)",        "DOW / Holiday"),
    ("Lag demand\n(t-24h, t-168h)", "Demand (E)"),
    ("Cooling",         "Demand (E)"),
    ("Heating",         "Demand (E)"),
    ("Inland heat",     "Demand (E)"),
    ("Heat persistence","Demand (E)"),
    ("Humidity effect", "Demand (E)"),
    ("Wind effect",     "Demand (E)"),
    ("Daily shape",     "Demand (E)"),
    ("Seasonal",        "Demand (E)"),
    ("Lighting",        "Demand (E)"),
    ("DOW / Holiday",   "Demand (E)"),
    ("Baseline (E0)",   "Demand (E)"),
    # Mediation path: weather -> lag -> demand
    ("Temperature",     "Lag demand\n(t-24h, t-168h)"),
]

G.add_nodes_from(nodes.keys())
G.add_edges_from(edges)

# Layout
pos = {
    "Hour (h)":         (-2,  3),
    "Month (m)":        ( 2,  3),
    "Temperature":      (-2,  1.5),
    "Humidity":         (-0.5, 1.5),
    "Wind":             ( 1,  1.5),
    "Solar radiation":  ( 2.5, 1.5),
    "Lag demand\n(t-24h, t-168h)": (-3.5, 0),
    "Cooling":          (-2.5, 0),
    "Heating":          (-1.8, 0),
    "Inland heat":      (-1.1, 0),
    "Heat persistence": (-0.4, 0),
    "Humidity effect":  ( 0.3, 0),
    "Wind effect":      ( 1.0, 0),
    "Daily shape":      ( 1.7, 0),
    "Seasonal":         ( 2.4, 0),
    "Lighting":         ( 3.1, 0),
    "DOW / Holiday":    ( 3.8, 0),
    "Baseline (E0)":    ( 4.5, 0),
    "Demand (E)":       ( 1,  -1.5),
}

color_map = {
    "exog":      "#4c72b0",
    "weather":   "#55a868",
    "lag":       "#c44e52",
    "component": "#dd8452",
    "output":    "#8172b2",
}

node_colors = [color_map[nodes[n]] for n in G.nodes()]

# Highlight mediation path
mediation_edges = {("Temperature", "Lag demand\n(t-24h, t-168h)")}
edge_colors = ["#c44e52" if e in mediation_edges else "#888888"  for e in G.edges()]
edge_widths = [2.5      if e in mediation_edges else 1.0        for e in G.edges()]

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(16, 8))
nx.draw_networkx(
    G, pos, ax=ax,
    node_color=node_colors, node_size=1800,
    font_size=7, font_color="white", font_weight="bold",
    edge_color=edge_colors, width=edge_widths,
    arrows=True, arrowsize=15,
    bbox=dict(boxstyle="round,pad=0.3", fc="none"),
)

legend_handles = [
    mpatches.Patch(color=c, label=l) for l, c in [
        ("Exogenous (h, m)",  "#4c72b0"),
        ("Weather",           "#55a868"),
        ("Lag demand",        "#c44e52"),
        ("Demand components", "#dd8452"),
        ("Total demand",      "#8172b2"),
    ]
]
ax.legend(handles=legend_handles, loc="lower left", fontsize=9)
ax.set_title("SCM Causal DAG — California Electricity Demand", fontsize=13)
ax.axis("off")

# Annotation for mediation path
ax.annotate("mediation path\n(weather → lag → demand)",
            xy=(-2.8, 0.75), fontsize=8, color="#c44e52",
            ha="center", style="italic")

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

SCM Structure¶

Exogenous roots¶

Hour (h), Month (m) — purely structural, no parents in the gr

Weather layer [h, m → weather]¶

Temperature is modeled with Fourier harmonics in hour and month. Humidity, Wind, Solar radiation follow the same seasonal patt

Demand components [weather → components → E]¶

Cooling / Heating linear HVAC response above/below learnable thresholds Inland extreme heat temp_max captures regional extremes dilut Heat persistence yesterday's inland max → overnight demand (thermal mass) Humidity effect active only when T > 70°F Wind effect heating-side below 50°F, cooling-side above 80°F Daily shape Fourier harmonics in hour-of-day Seasonal Fourier harmonics in month Lighting L0 · exp(−β · Rad) during daylight hours DOW / Holiday fixed effects Baseline (E0) intercept

Lag demand [E_{t-24h}, E_{t-168h} → E]¶

Weighted sum of yesterday and last-week same-hour demand. Acts as the level predictor; SCM terms model deviations. ⚠ Mediation path: Temperature → Lag → Demand During heatwaves, the lag carries elevated prior-day demand hot weather, so naïve decomposition over-attributes uplift to "Lag demand". Counterfactual lag analysis (section 8b) corrects for this.

Total demand¶

E = E_hvac + E_lighting + E_daily + E_seasonal + E_dow + E_holiday + E_lag

11 — Posterior parameter inspection¶

In [60]:
param_store = pyro.get_param_store()
key_params = ['k_cool', 'k_heat', 'E0', 'k_cool_sq', 'k_max', 'k_persist',
              'T_cool_base', 'T_heat_base', 'T_max_base', 'T_persist_base',
              'w_lag24', 'w_lag168', 'delta_holiday']
print(f"{'Parameter':<22}  {'loc':>10}  {'scale':>10}")
print('-' * 46)
for name in key_params:
    loc_key   = f'AutoNormal.locs.{name}'
    scale_key = f'AutoNormal.scales.{name}'
    if loc_key in param_store:
        loc   = param_store[loc_key].item()
        scale = param_store[scale_key].item() if scale_key in param_store else float('nan')
        print(f'  {name:<20}  {loc:>10.2f}  {scale:>10.4f}')
    else:
        print(f'  {name:<20}  (not in store)')

Parameter                      loc       scale
----------------------------------------------
  k_cool                (not in store)
  k_heat                (not in store)
  E0                    (not in store)
  k_cool_sq             (not in store)
  k_max                 (not in store)
  k_persist             (not in store)
  T_cool_base           (not in store)
  T_heat_base           (not in store)
  T_max_base            (not in store)
  T_persist_base        (not in store)
  w_lag24               (not in store)
  w_lag168              (not in store)
  delta_holiday         (not in store)

12 — Baseline comparison¶

In [61]:
baseline_path = RESULTS_DIR / 'baseline_mapes.csv'
if baseline_path.exists():
    baselines = pd.read_csv(baseline_path)
    scm_row = pd.DataFrame([{
        'model': 'SCM (this work)',
        'test_mape': test_pm['all_hour_mape'],
        'top1_mape': test_pm['top1_mape'],
        'monthly_peak_mape': test_pm['monthly_peak_mape'],
    }])
    comparison = pd.concat([baselines, scm_row], ignore_index=True)
    display(comparison.sort_values('test_mape'))
else:
    print(f'No baseline file found at {baseline_path}')
    print(f'Run model/baselines.py first, then re-run this cell.')
